textPlain: I wish we had croussants here in teh states (tiny speciality shops not withstanding). They are a rare. instead we get a weird butter flavored croissant shaped bread with nothing of the texture or aroma of the real thing.

Unfortunately, WebSmell-o-Vision has not yet been unilaterally pushed by the dudes at Google to do this news justice, but as a croissant connaisseur I would have at least appreciated a webp of the thing instead of some random archive image. It’s a stamp, for christ’s sake.There is a pic here and it’s quite nice imo: https://www.wopa-plus.com/en/stamps/product/&pid=105515

Nice, we got 4 fruits in Switzerland:
https://shop.post.ch/en/stamps-philately/philately/single-st...
Do they taste buttery when you lick them?
I am holding off for the red wine flavoured one.
Now this is the future I’ve been waiting for
La Poste might have invented the first non-vegan stamps in history.I hope they don't turn rancid.
Try and find a good one in France. Harder than you’d think
But wopa-plus does even mention the essential detail?https://www.laposte.fr/pp/c/timbre-croissant-au-beurre
I'm holding out for a <smell> HTML tag.
I miss my concrete stamps... A bit rough on the tongue but still lovely
Given the normal process for making glue I wouldn't be so certain.
> Try and find a good one in France. Harder than you’d thinkIn Paris? Sure. You're competing with tourists. Almost any rural bakery? No. You'd have to try to find something shitty.
